title: Physio Shared Care with Doctor, Nurse and Paramedic
synonyms: Physio Shared Care with Doctor, Nurse and Paramedic

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Physio care in FIFOEMS often overlaps with doctors, nurses, and paramedics. Shared care should be deliberate, role-aware, and centred on patient safety rather than parallel siloed treatment.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation
sections.content:
- Persistent pain with medication needs
- Concussion or vestibular symptoms with medical overlap
- Work capacity disputes or complex RTW planning
- Falls, frailty, chronic disease, or post-illness recovery requiring multiple disciplines

sections.title: Considerations
sections.content:
- Shared care works best when each clinician knows what decision they own
- Physio input should not drift into implicit medical diagnosis management when doctor review is clearly needed
- Repeated duplicated assessments frustrate patients and create mixed messages

sections.title: Management - Primary
sections.content:
- Seek early clinical discussion when pain control, medical stability, transport, or work risk is not straightforward
- Use direct handover rather than assuming another clinician will read the notes in time
- Escalate immediately if the patient becomes unstable while under physio review

sections.title: Management - Advanced
sections.content:
- Clarify the shared plan: diagnosis uncertainty, restrictions, medication, follow-up timing, and who will review deterioration
- Use structured handover and document advice received
- Ensure the patient knows who to contact next and under what circumstances

sections.title: Additional Notes
sections.content: - Shared care fails when the patient hears three different plans from three clinicians
